HomeBiographyMusicContact

"Music has been a part of my life since childhood.
I am thankful for all the opportunities and challenges that came with this calling."

Multicultural
Ensembles

Driven by the Accordion versatility, I have founded several musical ensembles like: Taraf Syriana, ROMada, POPAZA, Sergiu Popa Ensemble.
‍
My musical quest offered honourable collaborations with: Via Romen ensemble, Black Sea Cowboy, SoCalled, Ben Caplan, Wesli,Suzi Silva.

Accordion
Performance

I was engaged on multiple projects at the Dolly Parton's Dollywood Festival of Nations, the Grammy Award-nominated project Yiddish Glory and OktoEcho.

Sergiu Popa Ensemble introduced the opening concert for Emir Kusturica and No Smoking Orchestra at Montreal Jazz Festival.

Awards

Received the Creation Grant for the original work (Bijando) at the Festival of Word Music in Quebec. Winner of the Circuit 514 Prize for the artist-in-residence in Montreal.

Awarded the Radio-Canada Étoiles Galaxie Prize for musical director of production. Winner of the META award (Montreal English Theatre Award) for Outstanding contribution to Theatre.

Testimonials

Boris Dolinski

"Sergiu Popa was one of my best students. He was a disciplined and hard-working. His distinctive quality is a deep and subtle sense of folklore. He excellently performs folklore of different nations: Romanian, Bulgarian, Gypsy, Gagauz, Serbian, Ukrainian, Jewish and many others. I think that he should share his knowledge in a methodological work, which should be published for the general public. I am proud that I had such a talented student."

Boris Dolinski
Teacher at the Chisinau Music College named after St. Neaga. Moldova.

Carmen Piculeata

"Sergiu Popa is an accordionist with an extremely developed harmonic musical sense. We have collaborated in Montreal for many years with other renowned musicians. He specializes in Eastern European music."

Carmen Piculeata
Romanian Violonist Legend
